overview.short: Yeshwantgad also spelt as Yashwantgad is an island fortification off the coast of Maharashtra in Ratnagiri district.
overview.description: Yeshwantgad also spelt as Yashwantgad is an island fortification off the coast of Maharashtra in Ratnagiri district. It is built on the Rajapur creek with the sea on one side. On three sides it was protected by a ditch which can no longer be seen. On the fourth side there was a wall with 17 bastions.

topPlaces.name: Ambolgad
topPlaces.description: Ambolgad is a village near Jaitapur Nuclear Power Project in the Ratnagiri district in Maharashtra, India. Ambolgad has very scenic beach in Ratnagiri near Nate.

topPlaces.name: Jaitapur Nuclear Power Project
topPlaces.description: Jaitapur Nuclear Power Project is a proposed nuclear power plant in India. If built, it would be the largest nuclear power generating station in the world by net generation capacity, at 9,900 MW. As of 2025, each unit's power has been increased to 1730MW and the installed capacity has been raised to 10,380MW. The power project is proposed by Nuclear Power Corporation of India (NPCIL) and would be built at Madban village of Ratnagiri district in Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Vijaydurg (town)
topPlaces.description: Vijaydurg (formerly also known as Gheriah) is a seaport 485 km from Mumbai on India's West Coast. It boasts a safe harbour with average depths in excess of 100 feet.

topPlaces.name: Rameshwar Wadi
topPlaces.description: Rameshwar also known as Rameshwar Wadi is a small town located on the coast of Sindhudurg District of Maharashtra on the west coast of India. A very old Shri Dev Rameshwar Temple is located in this town which is dedicated to the Hindu deity Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Vijaydurg
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Vijaydurg was fought between Tulaji Angre, the Admiral of the Maratha Navy and the combined forces of the British East India Company and the Maratha Empire led by Peshwa Balaji Baji Rao in early 1756.

items.note: Ambolgad is a village near Jaitapur Nuclear Power Project in the Ratnagiri district in Maharashtra, India. Ambolgad has very scenic beach in Ratnagiri near Nate.

Jaitapur Nuclear Power Project is a proposed nuclear power plant in India. If built, it would be the largest nuclear power generating station in the world by net generation capacity, at 9,900 MW. As of 2025, each unit's power has been increased to 1730MW and the installed capacity has been raised to 10,380MW. The power project is proposed by Nuclear Power Corporation of India (NPCIL) and would be built at Madban village of Ratnagiri district in Maharashtra.
Vijaydurg (formerly also known as Gheriah) is a seaport 485 km from Mumbai on India's West Coast. It boasts a safe harbour with average depths in excess of 100 feet.

Rameshwar also known as Rameshwar Wadi is a small town located on the coast of Sindhudurg District of Maharashtra on the west coast of India. A very old Shri Dev Rameshwar Temple is located in this town which is dedicated to the Hindu deity Shiva.

The Battle of Vijaydurg was fought between Tulaji Angre, the Admiral of the Maratha Navy and the combined forces of the British East India Company and the Maratha Empire led by Peshwa Balaji Baji Rao in early 1756.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Yeshwantgad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Yeshwantgad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Kolhapur Airport (99 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (145 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Yeshwantgad?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Yeshwantgad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Yeshwantgad?
faq.a: Stays in Yeshwantgad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Yeshwantgad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ambolgad, Jaitapur Nuclear Power Project, Vijaydurg (town), Rameshwar Wadi, Battle of Vijaydurg.
